$comment: STATIC LIBRARY (`ar` archive of relocatable members) for pe64-x86_64-windows. c171 (D-FF1-AR-STATICLIB-DRIVER-WIRING). IDENTICAL to the `pe64-x86_64-windows` relocatable format -- its COFF x86_64 relocatable .obj members are written by the SAME relocatable writer + the same sections[]/relocations[] rows -- PLUS exactly `container: "archive"` + `artifactProfiles: ["staticlib"]` + the `-staticlib` name. The archive framing itself is FORMAT-BLIND (src/link/format/ar.cpp): the SysV `.a` armap for ELF/Mach-O, the Microsoft `.lib` 2nd linker member for PE (flavor threaded from kind() in linkAndWriteStaticArchive). No writer code is format-specific to the archive. Kept in sync with the relocatable sibling by hand: any section/reloc change there must mirror here.
$containerComment: D-FF1-AR-STATICLIB-DRIVER-WIRING (c171): `container: archive` marks this as a STATIC-LIBRARY format. The driver (compileOneTarget) dispatches on THIS field -- never the artifactProfile name (the artifact_profile.hpp veto) -- routing each CU's relocatable member (.obj) into ONE `ar` archive via linkAndWriteStaticArchive. This is the user's Option-1 format-container decision: output SHAPE stays 100% format-driven (cli->exec, lib->shared, staticlib->archive), exactly as elf.type / pe.type / macho.filetype select the single-file shapes.
$artifactProfilesComment: This format SERVES the `staticlib` artifact profile (plan 06). It is the FIRST format to declare it -- the bare relocatable sibling serves NO profile (a `.o` is an internal cross-CU intermediate / a static-archive member, never a project's final artifact). enforceArtifactProfileFormat validates a `staticlib` project against this set.
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit Windows is LLP64 (int 32, long 32, long long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I32 here, the literal ladder + descriptor signatureByDataModel resolve their LLP64 arms. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: MSVC x64 bit-field packing (byte-ABI-exact to cl.exe). The strategy is OS/format-determined (x86_64 serves both PE and ELF), so it lives on the format, not the target. See pe64-x86_64-windows-exec for the full rule.
$longDoubleFormatComment: FC17.9(e) D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE: MSVC x64 `long double` IS `double` (64-bit IEEE, 8/8) — the f64 axis collapses the c-subset `long double` row to F64. See pe64-x86_64-windows-exec for the full rationale.
$externCallDispatchComment: D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE (PE/COFF arm, c148 — the PE analog of the ELF c141 + Mach-O c146 closures): extern FUNCTION calls lower into this relocatable .obj as a plain direct `call rel32` (E8 disp32, zero patch bytes) + an IMAGE_REL_AMD64_REL32 relocation against an UNDEFINED COFF symbol (SectionNumber=0, StorageClass=EXTERNAL) carrying its REAL import name — PE x64 C mangling is IDENTITY (applyCMangling adds no leading underscore, unlike Mach-O), so `puts` is emitted verbatim. That rel32-CALL-against-undefined-symbol shape is the standard COFF contract: the FINAL linker (link.exe / lld-link) resolves it either directly to a same-link definition or through an import thunk it synthesizes for a DLL import — DSS never builds a stub/thunk inside a `.obj` (thunk synthesis is the EXEC walker's job, D-FFI-PE-IMPORT-THUNK). `direct-plt` is the declared verb because the call-site byte shape is the direct-call form (the ELF/Mach-O relocatable formats declare the same value for the same reason).
$supportedDataSectionsComment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE (PE/COFF arm, c148 — the PE analog of the ELF c142 + Mach-O c147 `.o` data sections): a relocatable .obj DOES carry data — a global lands in `.rdata`/`.data`/`.bss` with a section-relative IMAGE_SYMBOL (SectionNumber = the section's 1-based ordinal, Value = the item's SECTION-RELATIVE offset — the COFF convention, unlike Mach-O's flat-address n_value) the FINAL linker binds. Same format-blind `AssembledModule.dataItems` producer path as ELF/Mach-O. D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE (PE arm): a CONST global carrying LOAD-TIME relocations (a const function-pointer table / `int *const p = &x;` — sqlite's VFS method tables + `aSyscall[]`) lands in `relro` = a SECOND `.rdata`-named section (see the relro row) and the writer emits its OWN IMAGE_RELOCATION table (IMAGE_REL_AMD64_ADDR64, addend IN the 8-byte slot bytes — COFF's in-place-addend convention; IMAGE_RELOCATION has no addend column); a reloc-bearing MUTABLE pointer global lands in `data`. TLS (`tdata`/`tbss`) stays UNDECLARED — the linker's acceptsDataSection gate rejects it loud (the .obj walker emits no `.tls`/IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 machinery; that is the PE EXEC arm, D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL C3).

pe.$comment: IMAGE_FILE_HEADER identity. machine = IMAGE_FILE_MACHINE_AMD64 (0x8664). characteristics = 0 — relocatable .obj carries no file-level flags (link.exe sets image-level flags when producing the .exe/.dll).

sections.$comment: Executable code. PE Characteristics = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_CODE (0x20) | IMAGE_SCN_ALIGN_16BYTES (0x500000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_EXECUTE (0x20000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) = 0x60500020. PE's `type` field is repurposed to carry Characteristics (which encodes alignment via IMAGE_SCN_ALIGN_*BYTES bits); `flags` is unused; `addrAlign` MUST be 0 for PE rows (validate-rejected — PE encodes alignment in Characteristics, not the substrate's `addrAlign` field).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE (PE arm, c148): read-only initialized data (string literals, const globals WITHOUT relocations) — exactly where cl.exe places it in a .obj. Characteristics (`type`) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_INITIALIZED_DATA (0x40) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) = 0x40000040 = 1073741888 — DELIBERATELY WITHOUT IMAGE_SCN_ALIGN_*BYTES bits: the .obj walker ORs in the align class computed from the H1-raised layout alignment (max of every member item's alignment) at emit time, exactly the per-contribution align class cl.exe itself stamps (e.g. an 8-byte-aligned .rdata contribution carries 0x40400040). addrAlign MUST stay 0 (validate-rejected on PE rows — alignment lives in Characteristics, never the substrate field).
sections.name: .rdata

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE (PE arm, c148): writable initialized data (mutable globals, `int counter = 7;`). Characteristics (`type`) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_INITIALIZED_DATA (0x40) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE (0x80000000) = 0xC0000040 = 3221225536 — no align bits (walker-computed; see the rodata row). A reloc-bearing MUTABLE pointer global also lands here and the walker emits its IMAGE_RELOCATION records against this section (VirtualAddress = the slot's section-relative offset).
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE (PE arm, c148): zero-initialized data (tentative globals `int g;` — DSS commits tentatives to a real `.bss` definition, the gcc -fno-common shape, NOT the MSVC COMMON-symbol form). Characteristics (`type`) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_UNINITIALIZED_DATA (0x80) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE (0x80000000) = 0xC0000080 = 3221225600 — no align bits (walker-computed). COFF .obj header convention (cl.exe-witnessed, dumpbin-confirmed): the bss span lives in SizeOfRawData with PointerToRawData = 0 and VirtualSize = 0 — the FILE stores no bytes; the final linker reserves SizeOfRawData zero-fill bytes.
sections.name: .bss

sections.$comment: D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE (PE arm, c148): relocated-read-only const data — a CONST global carrying LOAD-TIME relocations (a const function-pointer table / `int *const p = &x;`). COFF has NO relro segment concept: MSVC places reloc-bearing const data in read-only `.rdata` and the LINKED image base-relocates it (the loader applies IMAGE_REL_BASED_DIR64 into the slot before the page is mapped read-only) — cl.exe-witnessed: `int (*const table[])(void)` lands in `.rdata` (0x40400040) with ADDR64 relocations. So this row is `.rdata`-CLASS under the SAME `.rdata` NAME but as its OWN row → the walker emits it as a SECOND `.rdata` section header, which COFF explicitly permits (link.exe merges same-name contributions into one image section). A distinct row (rather than folding into the rodata row) keeps the .o discipline the ELF `.data.rel.ro` / Mach-O `__DATA,__const` mirrors use: reloc-bearing rodata stays fail-loud while relro items get their own IMAGE_RELOCATION table. Characteristics (`type`) = 0x40000040 = 1073741888 — identical class to the rodata row, no align bits (walker-computed).
sections.name: .rdata

relocations.$comment: 32-bit PC-relative (call / jmp rel32). IMAGE_REL_AMD64_REL32 = 4. `kind` = 1 matches x86_64.target.json's `rel32` row. link.exe handles the rel32 RIP-bias intrinsically (PE convention: the patch site holds whatever bytes the assembler wrote; the linker computes `target_va - (section_va + VA + 4)` and ADDS the stored value). The assembler writes 0x00000000 at the patch site — addend=0 is the PE convention for call/jmp rel32.
relocations.name: IMAGE_REL_AMD64_REL32
